stories.headline: What We Know About the Los Angeles Warehouse Fire
stories.summary: Smoke spread across Los Angeles on Sunday, but the Fire Department said it was making progress in extinguishing the fire at the cold-storage warehouse. State and local officials have declared an emergency.

stories.headline: Far-right millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella wins Colombia’s presidential runoff
stories.summary: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer secures narrow majorityThe Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” Abelardo de la Espriella has won Colombia’s presidential runoff, defeating the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda.With 99.98% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, De la Espriella had secured 12.95m votes, or 49.66%, just 249,901 more than Cepeda, who received 12.7m

stories.headline: Malaysia's Anwar: All's well that ends well?
Qatar Rushes to Bring Back Empty LNG Ships as It Lifts Exports
stories.summary: Qatar is seeking to bring more empty liquefied natural gas tankers back home, as the nation aims to return about a fifth of global supply.

stories.headline: Pound Trades Near 2026 Low as UK Political Uncertainty Builds
stories.summary: The pound traded near this year’s low on expectations Keir Starmer will set out a timetable for his departure as UK prime minister in coming days.

stories.headline: Vale Board Resists Shareholder Previ’s Bid to Oust Chairman
stories.summary: Board members of Vale SA, the world’s top iron ore producer, voted against a proposal by one of the company’s largest shareholders to remove Daniel André Stieler as chairman, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Abaxx Calls for Probe, Hires Paul Weiss After Short Seller’s Attack
stories.summary: Abaxx Technologies Inc. is asking Canadian market regulators to investigate whether manipulative trading occurred in its shares, and has hired law firm Paul Weiss for help in response to a short-selling campaign by Viceroy Research.

stories.headline: US, Iran Meet in Switzerland as Trump Threatens Iran
stories.summary: The US and Iran began talks in Switzerland on a peace deal to settle the issue of the Islamic Republic’s nuclear program as President Donald Trump once again threatened strikes if Hezbollah keeps attacking Israel. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.headline: US and Iran publicly trade threats as talks take place in Switzerland
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to strike Iran if it did not stop Hezbollah from "causing trouble", while Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned the US to "be careful with their statements".

stories.headline: Russian-occupied Crimea suspends petrol sales amid fuel crisis
stories.summary: Kyiv's targeting of Russia's energy industry has sparked a major fuel crisis in Crimea, with long queues for petrol stations and limited supply.

stories.headline: Half of France under red heat alert as alcohol banned at street music festival
stories.summary: Temperatures are forecast to peak on Monday, and authorities warn they could match historic highs.

stories.headline: BTS fans desperate to catch comeback tour lose more than $100,000 to scammers
stories.summary: Elated to see BTS on the road again, but frustrated by competitive ticket wars, they have become easy targets.

stories.headline: Asia Stocks Rise, Oil Drops as Iran Talks Progress: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian stocks rallied while oil erased early gains as the US and Iran agreed upon a roadmap toward reaching a final peace deal within 60 days, with talks expected to continue for the rest of this week.

stories.headline: Bond Traders Burned by Fed’s Pivot Look to Prices Gauge, Oil
stories.summary: Bond traders, recently forced to reposition for the possibility of higher interest rates ahead, are looking to this week’s personal spending data for an early read on whether the market’s newly hawkish stance is warranted.

stories.headline: Colombia elections take place as Abelardo de la Espriella leads polls
stories.summary: Colombians are voting in an election where senator Ivan Cepeda is racing against Abelardo de la Espriella.

stories.headline: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike

stories.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
stories.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

stories.headline: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
stories.summary: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.
